item.title: Supreme Court expands presidential firing power, overturning 90-year-old ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court overturned a 90-year-old decision that allowed Congress to shield members of certain independent agencies from being fired by the president at will.

item.title: Afghanistan's Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 36 civilians
item.description: Pakistani security forces carried out an intelligence-based ground operation along the Pakistan-Afghanistan​ border, followed by "calibrated strikes."

item.title: U.S.-Iran talks to resume after exchange of strikes, Trump says
item.description: President Trump says U.S.-Iran talks will resume, at Tehran's request, after several days of tit-for-tat strikes tested a shaky ceasefire.

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear Trump's appeal of E. Jean Carroll sexual abuse case
item.description: A unanimous federal jury found that a preponderance of evidence supported Carroll's claim that Mr. Trump sexually abused her.

item.title: Ex-NBA players Malik Beasley, Ed Davis indicted in gambling probe
item.description: Former NBA players Malik Beasley and Ed Davis have been indicted on illegal sports gambling charges, authorities announced Monday.

item.title: Malik Beasley indicted in latest round of charges in DOJ sports gambling probe
item.description: Malik Beasley, who played in the NBA for nine years, was indicted Monday alongside former player Ed Davis in the latest round of charges by the Justice Department as part of its gambling investigation, officials said. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to fire Fed's Lisa Cook as legal case continues
item.description: The Supreme Court allowed Lisa Cook to continue in her post as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors while legal proceedings over President Trump's attempt to fire her continue.

item.title: Luigi Mangione due in federal court for pretrial hearing
item.description: Luigi Mangione is set to be in federal court on Monday for a pretrial hearing. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.

item.title: Inside Venezuela amid heroic earthquake rescue efforts
item.description: Venezuelans continue rescue efforts after two deadly earthquakes hit the South American country last week. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more.

item.title: 3 firefighters killed, at least 2 others injured battling wildfires in the Southwest
item.description: Three firefighters were killed, and at least 2 others sustained injuries, battling wildfires near the Utah-Colorado border. CBS News' Jonah Kaplan has the latest.

item.title: Supreme Court says states can count mail ballots that arrive after Election Day
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count mail ballots that are cast by Election Day but arrive later, rejecting a GOP challenge to a Mississippi law.

item.title: Supreme Court rules that mail-in ballots can be counted after Election Day in blow to GOP
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled Monday in a 5-4 decision that mail-in ballots can be counted if they're cast by Election Day, even if they arrive afterward, crushing a Republican challenge to a Mississippi law. CBS News' Major Garrett has the latest.

item.title: Woman killed, 2 injured in series of Florida alligator attacks
item.description: Officials say people and pets should keep a safe distance away from the water to limit the chances of an alligator encounter.

item.title: Reflecting on Patrick Henry's famous "Give Me Liberty or Give Me Death" speech
item.description: To celebrate 250 years of America, CBS News contributor David Begnaud shows how six top debate competitors from around the country joined together to recite parts of Patrick Henry's famous "Give Me Liberty or Give Me Death" speech. The fiery address is often credited for helping push the colonies toward revolution.

item.title: Should you use a credit card to pay for pet care?
item.description: Credit cards can bridge the budgetary gap when paying for vet care, but they're not the best long-term option.

item.title: Putin says Ukrainian strikes on infrastructure causing "problems" for Russia
item.description: Ukraine has intensified strikes on Russia recently, targeting energy and logistics infrastructure.

item.title: Supreme Court takes up dispute over Arizona voting restrictions
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed to decide whether Arizona can impose voting rules, including a measure that requires documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote on a state form.

item.title: Supreme Court turns away Alan Dershowitz's defamation case against CNN
item.description: The Supreme Court declined to take up former Harvard law professor Alan Dershowitz's case alleging CNN defamed him.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects case over COVID-19 vaccine mandate for healthcare workers
item.description: The dispute arose after New York's Department of Health issued an emergency rule that required healthcare workers to be fully vaccinated against COVID-19.

item.title: Side effects from extreme heat for people who use weight-loss drugs
item.description: More than 10% of Americans say they use GLP-1 drugs. But experts warn some side effects from the drugs could lead to dehydration, especially in extreme heat. Nutritionist Keri Glassman explains.

item.title: Supreme Court rules against Trump on Lisa Cook case, says Rebecca Slaughter's firing was lawful
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled Monday that President Trump cannot fire Fed governor Lisa Cook while legal proceedings continue, but that his firing of Federal Trade Commission member Rebecca Slaughter was lawful. CBS News' Jan Crawford and contributors Rebecca Roiphe and Jessica Levinson have the latest.

item.title: Olivia Wilde shares why she was initially reluctant to star in "The Invite"
item.description: Olivia Wilde and Edward Norton, stars of "The Invite" preview the film, which explores the unraveling and evolution of two very different marriages over the course of an unforgettable evening. The two discuss if they brought any of their own experiences to the movie and Wilde, who also directed the film, shares why she was initially reluctant to star in it.

item.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
item.description: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

item.title: Trailer for "The Angry Birds Movie 3" unveiled
item.description: "CBS Mornings" exclusively reveals the trailer for "The Angry Birds Movie 3." The film stars Jason Sudeikis and debuts in theaters on Dec. 23.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump's appeal in E. Jean Carroll sexual abuse case
item.description: The Supreme Court Monday rejected President Trump's appeal in the E. Jean Carroll sexual abuse case. CBS News legal contributor Rebecca Roiphe has the latest.

item.title: Supreme Court rules mail-in ballots cast by Election Day can be counted if they arrive later
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled Monday that mail-in ballots cast by Election Day but arrive after can still be counted. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and legal contributor Rebecca Roiphe have the latest.

item.title: Supreme Court rules against Trump as it releases term's final decisions | Special Report
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled against President Trump's bid to fire Lisa Cook, a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors, while legal proceedings over President Trump's attempt to fire her continue. The ruling comes as the highest court approaches the end of its term. Tony Dokoupil anchored this CBS News special report.

item.title: Comcast plans to split into 2 companies, spinning off NBCUniversal
item.description: Current shareholders would receive shares in both companies under the planned split, Comcast said Monday.

item.title: Former NFL star Chris Johnson says he's been diagnosed with ALS
item.description: Former NFL running back Chris Johnson announced that he was diagnosed with ALS, also known as Lou Gehrig's disease, in a "Good Morning America" interview.

item.title: 3 firefighters killed, 2 injured fighting wildfires near Colorado-Utah border
item.description: The U.S. Wildland Fire Service said the firefighters had been part of an interagency response to the Knowles and Gore fires near the Colorado-Utah border.
